missing_holes.summary: Incorrect registration date '1 January 2026' for PROS-005 and PROS-007. Dome source: 2026-03-06 for both. No Jan 2026 registration exists.

The 'Credit where due: this is the closest any dome prediction comes to genuine temporal priority' concession is based on the wrong registration date. Under the correct date (2026-03-06), ~99% of the 12-month decay window had elapsed at registration — the observation was essentially complete. This r
'Logically entailed by WIN-007' overstates — WIN-007 predicts NP acceleration rate, not position. The arithmetic that 139.3°E is 19.3° from 120°E is trivial but not entailed by WIN-007's rate claim.

'Suggesting the value was adopted from literature without understanding the oscillating nature of the signal' is speculative editorial glossing. The sign/magnitude mismatch (−1.0 ±0.5 predicted vs. +1-2 observed) is a real problem for the dome, but the 'without understanding' claim is unsupported an
'74 years after its discovery' conflates Schumann's 1952 theoretical prediction with the actual first observation (Balser & Wagner 1960).
